name: CHANGELOG
info:
FLEET MANAGER — CHANGELOG
==========================

v34 | 2026-04-07
- Output box added to all 4 playbook-triggering modals:
  Deploy flow, Deploy Wi-Fi, Deploy settings, Cache actions
  Shows ansible output after run; button changes to Close on success

v32 | 2026-04-07
- Devices page: APT and NPM columns removed from table
- Devices page: Refresh status button hidden
- loadFacts no longer updates APT/NPM cells (hostname/MAC/OS still fetched)

v31 | 2026-04-07
- Fixed: Delete APT package was not removing name from packages.yml because
  deleteApt() only sent {file}, not {file, name} to the DELETE endpoint

v30 | 2026-04-07
- Fixed: List NPM cache was showing APT packages because it read all list items
  from packages.yml without scoping to the npm_packages section

v28 | 2026-04-07
- APT Cache table now only shows explicitly added packages (reads apt_packages
  from vars/packages.yml, same pattern as NPM cache)
- Download APT package appends name to apt_packages in packages.yml
- Delete APT package removes name from apt_packages in packages.yml
- install_apt.yml should use vars_files: packages.yml and apt_packages var

v25c | 2026-04-07
- NPM cache package list now persisted in vars/packages.yml instead of flow context
- List NPM cache reads npm_packages from packages.yml
- Cache NPM package appends to packages.yml on successful cache
- Delete NPM package removes from packages.yml on delete

v25b | 2026-04-07
- Removed /-/all fallback from List NPM cache (Verdaccio returns all proxied
  packages incl. transitive deps, not just top-level)
- List now shows empty if flow context is empty — re-add packages to populate

v25 | 2026-04-07
- NPM Cache table now only shows user-added packages, not transitive dependencies
- List NPM cache: reads from flow context (npmCachePackages); on first load with no
  context, falls back to Verdaccio HTTP API (/-/all) and seeds context from result
- Cache NPM package: stores package name in flow context on success
- Delete NPM package: removes package name from flow context on success

v24 | 2026-04-07
- Install NPM: GUI now passes npm_package_list=<pkg1>,<pkg2> from the NPM Cache table
  instead of letting the playbook query all Verdaccio storage (avoids transitive deps)

v22 | 2026-04-07
- Cache page: toolbar with Install APT and Update NPM buttons (no proxy buttons)
- Control Pi IP dropdown in modals, populated from control Pi network interfaces
  (os.networkInterfaces() injected as {{{controlPiIPs}}} at page load)
- control_pi_ip passed as extra var to install_apt.yml and update_npm.yml
- Removed Point APT+NPM and Reset APT+NPM from cache page
  (still available in Run playbook tab)
- update_npm.yml should use path:/home/pi/.node-red, become_user:pi, registry var
- install_apt.yml should use apt proxy var instead of hardcoded proxy config

v21 | 2026-04-07
- WiFi page: Deploy button added to toolbar, opens target modal (fleet/group/device)
- Flows tab renamed to NR-Flows
- NR-Settings tab added: CRUD on /home/pi/fleet-management/files/*.js|json
  - List, preview, create (paste/drag-drop), rename, edit, delete
  - Deploy modal targets fleet/group/device via deploy_nodered_settings.yml
  - GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /fleet/settings endpoints added

v20 | 2026-04-07
- Fixed: ANSIBLE_CONFIG env var prepended to all Ansible commands in Build ansible
  command node and get facts node so Node-RED picks up ansible.cfg regardless of cwd
- Fixed: --one-line flag removed from setup command (deprecated)
- get facts parser updated to handle multi-line JSON output using brace-depth tracking

v19 | 2026-04-07
- Fixed: removeDevice function missing, Del button on Devices page was broken
- Fixed: console.log not applied to showToast in this file (re-applied)

v18 | 2026-04-07
- console.log added to showToast and safeToast for easier traceability
- Format: [toast] <msg> or [toast] [ERROR] <msg>

v17 | 2026-04-07
- Restored missing openAddModal / closeAddModal / submitAddDevice functions
- Add device button on Devices page was broken due to missing functions

v16 | 2026-04-07
- Deploy flow modal: switched extra var from target_group/target_device to flow_file=<full path>
- Added All devices (fleet) option to deploy target dropdown
- Fixed :: separator to avoid conflicts with device names containing colons
- Playbook deploy_flows.yml simplified to accept flow_file var directly, no filename matching

v15 | 2026-04-07
- Deploy selected on Flows page now opens a modal instead of a confirm dialog
- Target dropdown populated from live inventory: All devices, Groups, Devices

v14 | 2026-04-07
- Flows page: full CRUD operations added
  - Create: modal with dir selector, filename, drag-and-drop or paste JSON
  - Rename: modal pre-filled with current filename
  - Edit JSON: modal loads current content, supports drag-and-drop replacement
  - Delete: confirm dialog with safety guard on path
- POST / PUT / DELETE /fleet/flows Node-RED endpoints added

v13 | 2026-04-07
- Flows page: visual preview replaced canvas renderer with FlowFuse flow-renderer (index.min.js)
- Script loaded via httpStatic from /home/pi/fleet-management/public/flow-renderer/index.min.js
- Fallback error message shown if FlowRenderer not loaded

v12 | 2026-04-07
- Added Flows tab to nav bar
- Flows page: file browser (groups/devices), visual preview using FlowFuse flow-renderer
- GET /fleet/flows endpoint added (list files + read single file)
- Deploy selected button fires deploy_flows.yml with limit

v11 | 2026-04-07
- Restored 7 missing functions from flows__7__:
  openEdit, closeEdit, saveEdit, openSendCommandModal,
  closeSendCommandModal, submitSendCommand, rebootDevice

v10 | 2026-04-07
- Removed pre-cache packages from run playbook dropdown
- Install APT package: vars now Optional (installs all pre-cached by default)
- Combined Point APT + NPM to control Pi into single option (configure_proxy_registry.yml)
- Combined Reset APT + NPM to default into single option (reset_proxy_registry.yml)
- Fixed extra_vars key mismatch in runPlaybook fetch payload (extra -> extra_vars)

v9 | 2026-04-07
- Groups page: removed Move devices button
- Groups page: device chips are now draggable to reassign between groups
- safeToast added as fallback for drag-and-drop handlers where showToast may not be in scope

v8 | 2026-04-07
- Groups page: Members column added showing device chips per group
- Groups page: description persistence fixed using Node-RED flow context
  (was in-memory only, wiped on reload)
- Read inventory.ini node now injects groupDescriptions alongside inventory
- Add group API now accepts and stores desc via flow.set

v7 | 2026-04-07
- Run playbook page: extra vars replaced with key/value table (checkbox, key, value per row)
- Run playbook page: output moved to right side (side-by-side layout)

v6 | 2026-04-07
- Devices page: Send command button added per row
- Send command modal: shell input, output box, runs via Ansible shell module (__shell__ playbook)
- /fleet/run handler updated to support __shell__ command routing

v5 | 2026-04-07
- Devices page: bulk CSV import added (toolbar button, modal, preview, sequential POST)
- Supported columns: group, ip, hostname

v4 | 2026-04-07
- Devices page: Reboot button added per row (amber, confirm dialog)
- rebootDevice function fires reboot.yml limited to that device
- btn-warning CSS added
- Variables column removed from devices table

v3 | 2026-04-07
- Groups page redesigned to match Devices page pattern:
  toolbar + full-width card table, + Add group modal
- Set hostname removed from Run playbook dropdown
- Set hostname field added to Edit device modal (runs set_hostname.yml on save)
- saveEdit updated to fire hostname playbook if field filled

INFRASTRUCTURE NOTES
====================
Control Pi:   Raspberry Pi OS 64-bit, Node-RED direct install, port 1880
Fleet UI:     http://<control-pi-ip>:1880/fleet
Uptime Kuma:  port 3001
Verdaccio:    port 4873, storage /verdaccio/storage/data/
apt-cacher-ng: port 3142, cache /var/cache/apt-cacher-ng/
Inventory:    /home/pi/fleet-management/inventory.ini
Playbooks:    /home/pi/fleet-management/playbooks/
Flows dir:    /home/pi/fleet-management/flows/
Static files: /home/pi/fleet-management/public/ (httpStatic in settings.js)
